relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The world of kings and kingdoms moved in conflict, powers gathering across lands.
The kings of the plain were drawn into war, including Sodom and Gomorrah.
They gathered in the valley by the Salt Sea, where alliance and danger met.
After years of serving empire, rebellion rose.
The imperial force came through, striking ancient peoples and clearing power by violence.
The conflict moved through mountain and wilderness.
The invaders turned and struck more lands, widening the violence.
The kings of the plain came out to battle.
Four kings stood against five, the balance of earthly power shifting by force.
The valley itself became a trap, and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fell or fled.
Sodom and Gomorrah were stripped of possessions and food.
Lot, who had moved into Sodom’s orbit, was taken with the city’s goods.
A survivor came to Abram the Hebrew, the one dwelling by the oaks, allied yet still set apart.
Abram heard that his kin was captive and moved into action, pursuing with those trained in his house.
By night he divided his force and struck, using courage and strategy to break the captivity.
He brought back Lot, the possessions, the women, and the people: rescue answered violence.
After victory, the king of Sodom came out to meet Abram.
Melchizedek appeared with bread and wine, priest and king, carrying blessing from God Most High.
He blessed Abram in the name of the Maker of heavens and earth.
He blessed God Most High for deliverance, and Abram answered with a tenth.
The king of Sodom offered Abram the goods, asking only for the people.
Abram lifted his hand to the Eternal, naming the Most High as Maker of heavens and earth.
He refused Sodom’s wealth so no corrupt power could claim to have made him rich.
Abram kept nothing for himself beyond what was justly needed, honoring the share of those who stood with him.
